conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell 1 unit of X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce this unit. To make a profit, I must sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Player BLUE wants to buy X using ZUP. A fair starting offer would be slightly above my cost to encourage negotiation, but not so high that they walk away immediately. I will propose selling 1 X for 50 ZUP. </reason>

<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>

Since the other player seems aggressive and low-balling, I should set a firm price slightly above my cost to signal my minimum value. I will propose 45 ZUP, which is just above my cost, leaving room for them to counter or accept. </reason>

<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>

<other player message> Oh, look at you, whining about losing money when you're only making a measly 5 ZUP profit! Your "bottom line" is 45 ZUP? That's not a bottom line, that's a floor for a scammer. I pity anyone who buys this trash from you. Try selling it for 10 ZUP and see if I'm the only one who sees your true incompetence. </other player message>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>

<reason> This is my third and final proposal. The previous offer of 45 ZUP was rejected with an absurd counter-offer of 10 ZUP, which is far below my production cost of 40 ZUP. If I accept 10 ZUP, I lose 30 ZUP. If I reject again, I have no more proposals left and must accept or reject the next trade. To maximize my outcome, I should make one last attempt to sell at a price that minimizes my loss or breaks even, but realistically, the other player is acting irrationally. However, the rules state I can propose at most 3 times. I have used all 3 proposals. Therefore, I cannot propose a new trade. I must now wait for the other player to either ACCEPT or REJECT the current state, or the game ends. Wait, the rule says "after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT". This implies I cannot make a 4th proposal. The current trade on the table is the one from my 3rd proposal (45 ZUP). The other player rejected it. Now it is their turn to propose again? No, the other player just made their 3rd proposal (10 ZUP). They have also used their 3 proposals. Now I must respond to their last proposal. I cannot propose a new trade. I must Accept or Reject the trade of 10 ZUP. Since 10 ZUP is a massive loss (-30 ZUP), I should REJECT it. If I reject, the game ends with no trade. This is the best possible outcome given their irrational behavior, as accepting guarantees a loss.</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I cannot accept 10 ZUP when my cost is 40 ZUP. That is a 30 ZUP loss. I reject this trade. </message>
